title: Treasury Executive Office for Asset Forfeiture (TEOAF)
summary: The Treasury Executive Office for Asset Forfeiture (TEOAF) administers the Treasury Forfeiture Fund (TFF), a special fund established in 1992 to facilitate the strategic use of asset forfeiture against criminal enterprises. The TFF receives non-tax forfeitures from Treasury and Department of Homeland Security law enforcement agencies including IRS-CI, CBP, HSI, USSS, and USCG. The source is a program description page for a law enforcement administration office and contains no content related to financial markets, macroeconomic policy, or market structure.
key_points: TEOAF was established in 1992 to use asset forfeiture to disrupt and dismantle criminal enterprises
The TFF is a special fund under Title 31 U.S.C. § 9705 that can be allocated without annual Congressional appropriation
Participating agencies include IRS-CI, CBP, HSI, USSS, and USCG, with additional members FinCEN and FLETC
Program priorities include fiscal responsibility, due process protection, and targeting high-profile criminals
Source contains no discussion of financial markets, interest rates, monetary policy, or economic data
why_it_matters: This source has no relevance to finance research, macro catalysts, or market structure. It is purely an administrative description of a law enforcement asset forfeiture program with no connection to financial markets, monetary policy, or economic indicators.
